next_step_text: 6/19/26 MQ Alec notified me that we are one of the vendors qualified to participate in the RFP phase. RFP is slated to be issued by 7/10. 6/12/23 MQ RFI submitted. Qualified vendors will be notified of their inclusion in RFP phase. 6/3/26. MQ Initial call completed. Opportunity is Qualified. Renuity currently relies on manual processes across all procurement functions. They are a PE backed company

our_open_deliverables.text: Send conference invitation details for September Zycus conference to Alec Diaz

buyer_dependent_items.text: Send drafted RFI to Zycus by end of day 3 June 2026
Issue RFP to shortlisted vendors (including Zycus) by 10 July 2026

competitive_position.summary: Competitive multi-vendor RFI/RFP with 3 vendors to be shortlisted; the other rivals are not yet named in any evidence. The current single threat is being out-positioned on AI/ease-of-use or price during the RFP and demo. Zycus's wedge is its single-code-base, AI-native suite (Merlin intake in Teams, Anna negotiation agent, AP Smartdesk, iRisk) versus competitors who bolt on acquired modules.

competitors.name: Competitor 1 (unnamed)
competitors.quote: Renuity will select 3 vendors to participate in subsequent RFP.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin Intake + Agentic AI as the wedge for PE-backed decentralized procurement. Emphasize speed to value and ease of integration across acquired entities. Make AI the Phase 1 centerpiece in demos.

competitors.name: Competitor 2 (unnamed)
competitors.quote: Renuity will select 3 vendors to participate in subsequent RFP.
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin Intake + Agentic AI as the wedge for PE-backed decentralized procurement. Emphasize speed to value and ease of integration across acquired entities. Make AI the Phase 1 centerpiece in demos.

competitors.name: Unnamed alternative S2P vendors (RFI/RFP field)
competitors.quote: to be transparent, obviously, looking at other solutions as well
competitors.how_we_win: Land the single-code-base AI story (Merlin intake in Teams, Anna autonomous negotiation, AP Smartdesk) and contrast clean native integration vs competitors' bolt-on integration pain during the RFP and demo.

champion_strength.summary: Alec Diaz owns the evaluation, invited Zycus in, and is engaged on product detail, but is openly evaluating alternatives, does not control budget, and his advocacy for Zycus specifically is unproven.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic decision sits above Alec Diaz with the COO and the ELT, and Alec described the approval path clearly on the 3 Jun 2026 call: the COO tasked him to 'do an RFP... vet it down into one vendor and kinda come up with the business case and ROI, present it out to the ELT, and then get the buy-in and approval from them.' So the budget/approval authority is identified by role (COO sponsor, ELT approver) but neither the COO nor any ELT member is named, has joined a call, or is engaged. Alec runs the evaluation but does not control the budget. The move is to get the COO named and into the room before the business case lands, since the deal is won or lost on ELT buy-in he does not yet own.

recommended_moves: Deliver the RFP templates and follow-up letter to Alec Diaz this week.
Book the solution-consultant discovery session with Alec ahead of the demo.
Build a quantified indirect-spend ROI model with Alec for the ELT business case.
